title: Controversy grows over AI use in psychological counseling and therapy
summary: The use of artificial intelligence in psychological counseling and therapy is gaining momentum, but many experts remain concerned about its effectiveness and ethical implications. - VnExpress International

title: FAME-3 study reveals similar outcomes for CABG and PCI in severe triple-vessel heart disease
summary: In sharp contrast to earlier studies, patients with severe triple-vessel heart disease fared equally well whether they underwent open-heart bypass surgery (CABG) or a less invasive procedure called PCI (percutaneous coronary intervention) at five years of follow-up, according to research presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session.

title: Ibuprofen suppresses sweet taste perception
summary: Researchers explore how taste receptors react to sweet stimuli following exposure to ibuprofen.

title: Study links long-term antidepressant use to increased risk of sudden cardiac death
summary: A new study presented at the European Society of Cardiology's EHRA 2025 congress reveals a concerning link between the use of antidepressants (AD) and an increased risk of sudden cardiac death (SCD), particularly for those with prolonged use of the medication.

title: Michigan Transplant Patient Dies After Contracting…
summary: The donor, exposed to a wild animal weeks before their death, passed away without showing typical rabies symptoms and did not seek medical attention.

title: Antidepressant use associated with higher risk of sudden cardiac death in all age groups
summary: Sudden cardiac death (SCD) refers to an unexpected death of a person, believed to be caused by a heart-related issue. It occurs within one hour of the onset of symptoms in witnessed cases or within 24 hours of the person being last seen alive in unwitnessed cases.

title: Atrial fibrillation elevates early-onset dementia risk in younger adults
summary: New research presented at the EHRA 2025, a scientific congress of the European Society of Cardiology, shows that the presence of atrial fibrillation (AF) increases the risk of future dementia by 21% in patients diagnosed with AF under 70 and the risk of early-onset dementia (diagnosed before age 65 years) by 36%.

title: Iloilo launches weekly clean-up drive to combat dengue
summary: Iloilo — In a bid to curb the rising cases of dengue, Iloilo Governor Arthur Defensor Jr. issued an executive order implementing "Limpyo Iloilo, Kada

title: Parents urged to vaccinate young children against the flu as cases begin rising in SA
summary: More than 2,300 cases of the flu have been recorded in SA so far this year — 690 more than the same time last year — and the government is urging parents with young children to get their free vaccination.

title: Common Painkiller May Increase Risk-Taking, Study Finds
summary: Acetaminophen, widely known as Tylenol or Panadol, is one of the most frequently used pain relievers globally.2020 study suggests it may also influence behavior by increasing risk-taking tendencies.
